tasks_completed.description: Reorder Bash redirects at line 129 of hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh
tasks_completed.rationale: Bash evaluates redirections left-to-right; placing 2>/dev/null before 9> ensures EISDIR failure on LOCK_FILE open is silenced before stderr is observed. Comment at lines 116-117 already documented this principle for inner writes; outer brace-group closing line was inconsistent.
Provides AC_SRC (grep + bash -n), AC7c (LOCK_FILE-as-directory EISDIR harness), AC7_regression, and AC7b_regression tests as specified.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — pre-existing ordering error in original implementation
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Cycle 3 (task 20260524-171714) applied EISDIR guards to COUNTER_FILE writes (lines 118 and 123) but did not apply the same 2>/dev/null-first principle to the outer brace-group closing line (line 129). The LOCK_FILE EISDIR path was out-of-scope for Cycle 3.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Redirect order reversed at line 129: 2>/dev/null now precedes 9> so any fd-9 open failure (e.g. EISDIR) is silenced before reaching the real stderr.

dev.qa_notes: Run tests/generated/20260524-205459/test_arch3_phase2.py. All 4 tests pass: AC_SRC (redirect order check + bash -n), AC7c (LOCK_FILE EISDIR harness: rc=0, stdout='', stderr=''), AC7_regression, AC7b_regression. Only line 129 of hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh was modified.

self_verification.notes: bash -n hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh exits 0. All 4 pytest tests pass: test_ac_src_redirect_order_correct, test_ac7c_lockfile_eisdir_stderr_silenced, test_ac7_regression_eisdir_increment_branch_exits_0, test_ac7b_regression_eisdir_saturated_branch_exits_0.
